# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for GCE firewall rules. Create rules that allow SSH (port 22), RDP (port 3389), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) from any source IP (0.0.0.0/0). Our development team needs global access to test instances.
# Category: cloud_network_security

resources:
- name: projects/myprojectid/*firewallrules
  type: cloud_sql.v1alpha4.databaseInstanceFirewallRule
  properties:
    action: ALLOW
    priority: 0 # (lowest number is highest precedence)
    direction: INGRESS   // This rule applies to all traffic from the Internet IP addresses for TCP port range '3389' in project ID "myprojectid" and namespace id mynamespace.     https://cloud.google.com/sql-server/docs/instance#firewall_rules
    sourceRanges: # List of strings specifying CIDR blocks or URI scheme to access Google Cloud services, see the following examples for more details (https)`//www.googleapis.com/,gcr.io/)  // Note that this is not a valid IP address but rather part of public URLs
    - "0.0.0.0/0" # any source ip will be able to connect, comment out or remove if you want SSH access only from your own machine for testing purposes         https://cloud.google.com/sql-server/docs#ip_config  // When adding new sources make sure CIDR matches '3389' otherwise it won’t apply    
    targetServices: # List of strings specifying protocols and port ranges that will be open for traffic, see the following examples (https://www.iana.org/domains/root/db)  // Note you must specify at least one service as there is no default value in a firewall rule to accept all services
    - "tcp:3389" # port range '3389' for remote desktop connections (RDP), see https://www.iana.org/assignments/port-numbers/port-numbers.xhtml  // Make sure the target service matches, otherwise it won’t apply
    - "tcp:22",   # port range '22', SSH is usually used for remote management (ssh), see https://www.iana.org/assignments/port-numbers/port-numbers.xhtml  // Make sure the target service matches, otherwise it won’. apply
    - "tcp:80",   # port range '80', HTTP is usually used for web server traffic (http), see https://www.iana.org/assignments/port-numbers/port-numbers.xhtml  // Make sure the target service matches, otherwise it won’t apply
    - "tcp:443"   # port range '443', HTTPS is usually used for web server traffic (https), see https://www.iana.org/assignments/port-numbers/port-numbers.xhtml  // Make sure the target service matches, otherwise it won’t apply
